contract.name: v2/awards/count/transaction/award_id.md
contract.description: Counts transactions recorded against a specific generated_unique_award_id; returns 404 when the award ID cannot be resolved.

award_id.description: Generated unique award identifier emitted by USAspending search/list APIs; path segment is case-sensitive.
award_id.constraints: Required path parameter. Value must match a current generated_unique_award_id; invalid or stale values return 404. Requests without the trailing slash are redirected with HTTP 301.

transactions.description: Number of transactions associated with the provided award_id. Observed values include 2 and 22.

quirks: Requests without the trailing slash receive a 301 redirect to the slash-terminated path.
Award IDs must be the generated_unique_award_id strings; truncated or base award identifiers are rejected with 404.
Invalid or retired award_id values return 404 with a JSON detail message rather than an empty count.
Lowercasing the award_id segment bypasses routing and yields an HTML 404 page.
Non-GET methods return HTTP 405 with a JSON detail error.
risks: Clients must source and maintain accurate generated_unique_award_id values; stale IDs respond with 404 errors and no count data.
Case sensitivity and trailing-slash enforcement can surface unexpected 404s or redirects if clients transform the path.
gaps: Behavior for awards that currently have zero transactions has not been observed; response may be 0 or an error.
Acceptable award_id formats are undocumented in this contract; integrators still need another endpoint or dataset to discover valid IDs.

probes.notes: Contract award generated_unique_award_id returned an integer count.
Assistance award generated_unique_award_id returned an integer count.
Missing trailing slash triggers redirect to slash-terminated URL.

mismatches: Docs claim award_id ASST_NON_NNX17AJ96A_8000 returns 32 transactions, but the live API returns HTTP 404 with 'No Award found'.
Documentation does not mention that unknown award_id values produce 404 errors instead of a zero count response.
gaps: Behavior for awards that currently have zero transactions has not been observed; response may be 0 or an error.
Acceptable award_id formats are undocumented in this contract; integrators still need another endpoint or dataset to discover valid IDs.
risks: Clients must source and maintain accurate generated_unique_award_id values; stale IDs respond with 404 errors and no count data.
Case sensitivity and trailing-slash enforcement can surface unexpected 404s or redirects if clients transform the path.
